---
title: "Untitled"
format: html
---

# Load and clean data

## Loading data

```{python}
from pathlib import Path
import pandas as pd
import geopandas as gpd
import altair as alt
import json

# Set data path
DATA_DIR = Path("../data/derived-data")

# Crime
crime_raw = pd.read_csv(DATA_DIR / "Crimes_-_2001_to_Present_20260304.csv")
print("Crime loaded:", crime_raw.shape)

# Education
edu_raw = pd.read_csv(DATA_DIR / "ACSDT5Y2024.B15003-Data.csv")
print("Education loaded:", edu_raw.shape)

# Unemployment rate
unemp_raw = pd.read_csv(DATA_DIR / "ACSST5Y2024.S2301-Data.csv")
print("Unemployment loaded:", unemp_raw.shape)

# Income
income_raw = pd.read_csv(DATA_DIR / "ACSDT5Y2024.B19013-Data.csv")
print("Income loaded:", income_raw.shape)

# Tract shapefile
tract_raw = gpd.read_file(DATA_DIR / "tl_2023_17_tract" / "tl_2023_17_tract.shp")
print("Tract loaded:", tract_raw.shape)
```

## Cleaning data

### Cleaning crime data

```{python}
# Clean crime data
crime = crime_raw[[
    "Date",
    "Primary Type",
    "Year",
    "Latitude",
    "Longitude"
]].copy()
crime_raw.head()
```

### Cleaning education data

```{python}
# Remove the first row (metadata row)
edu = edu_raw.iloc[3:][[
    "GEO_ID",
    "B15003_001E",
    "B15003_021E",
    "B15003_022E",
    "B15003_023E",
    "B15003_024E"
]].copy()

# Extract tract_id
edu["tract_id"] = edu["GEO_ID"].str[-11:]

# Convert to numeric
cols = [
    "B15003_001E",
    "B15003_021E",
    "B15003_022E",
    "B15003_023E",
    "B15003_024E"
]

edu[cols] = edu[cols].apply(pd.to_numeric, errors="coerce")

# Bachelor+ population
edu["bachelor_plus"] = (
    edu["B15003_021E"] +
    edu["B15003_022E"] +
    edu["B15003_023E"] +
    edu["B15003_024E"]
)

# Bachelor+ rate
edu["edu_rate"] = edu["bachelor_plus"] / edu["B15003_001E"]

# Keep only needed columns
edu = edu[["tract_id", "edu_rate"]]

print("Education cleaned:", edu.shape)
display("Education table:", edu.head())
```

### Cleaning unemployment data

```{python}
# Clean unemployment data
unemp = unemp_raw.iloc[3:][[
    "GEO_ID",
    "S2301_C04_001E"
]].copy()

# Extract tract id
unemp["tract_id"] = unemp["GEO_ID"].str[-11:]

# Convert to numeric
unemp["S2301_C04_001E"] = pd.to_numeric(
    unemp["S2301_C04_001E"],
    errors="coerce"
)

# Rename column
unemp = unemp.rename(
    columns={"S2301_C04_001E": "unemployment_rate"}
)

# Keep final columns
unemp = unemp[[
    "tract_id",
    "unemployment_rate"
]]

print("Unemployment cleaned:", unemp.shape)
display("Unemployment table:", unemp.head())
```

### Cleaning income data

```{python}
# Clean income data
income = income_raw.iloc[3:][[
    "GEO_ID",
    "B19013_001E"
]].copy()

# Extract tract id
income["tract_id"] = income["GEO_ID"].str[-11:]

# Convert to numeric
income["B19013_001E"] = pd.to_numeric(
    income["B19013_001E"],
    errors="coerce"
)

# Rename column
income = income.rename(
    columns={"B19013_001E": "median_income"}
)

# Keep final columns
income = income[[
    "tract_id",
    "median_income"
]]

print("Income cleaned:", income.shape)
display("Income table:", income.head())
```

## Subset data for only Chicago tracts

### Tracts Geodata: keep only Chicago tract IDs

```{python}
# Load tract and Chicago boundary shapefiles 
tracts = gpd.read_file(DATA_DIR / "tl_2023_17_tract" / "tl_2023_17_tract.shp")
chicago = gpd.read_file(DATA_DIR / "Boundaries - City_20260303" / "geo_export_06d21f1c-6fda-4eae-9de6-a54e6cd59005.shp")

# Align CRS
chicago = chicago.to_crs(tracts.crs)

# Keep tracts that intersect Chicago boundary (without cutting geometry)
tracts_chi = gpd.sjoin(
    tracts,
    chicago[["geometry"]],
    how="inner",
    predicate="intersects"
).drop(columns=["index_right"])

# Ensure tract id format
tracts_chi["GEOID"] = tracts_chi["GEOID"].astype(str).str.zfill(11)

# Chicago tract id set
chi_tract_ids = set(tracts_chi["GEOID"])
print(f"Total tracts in Chicago: {len(chi_tract_ids)}")
```

### Crime Geodata (keep only Chicago crime data)

```{python}
# Make crime GeoDataFrame (Chicago only)
crime_geo = crime.dropna(subset=["Latitude", "Longitude"]).copy()

crime_gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
    crime_geo,
    geometry=gpd.points_from_xy(crime_geo["Longitude"], crime_geo["Latitude"]),
    crs="EPSG:4326"
)

tracts_chi_4326 = tracts_chi.to_crs("EPSG:4326")

crime_tract = gpd.sjoin(
    crime_gdf,
    tracts_chi_4326[["GEOID", "geometry"]],
    how="inner",
    predicate="within"
).rename(columns={"GEOID": "tract_id"})

crime_tract["tract_id"] = crime_tract["tract_id"].astype(str).str.zfill(11)

crime_tract.head()
```

# Part 1: Crime counts amoung tracts and income

## Relationship: Crime counts and Tracts

```{python}
# Count crimes per tract
crime_counts = (
    crime_tract.groupby("tract_id")
    .size()
    .reset_index(name="crime_count")
)

# Merge counts onto Chicago tracts
tracts_plot = tracts_chi.to_crs("EPSG:4326").copy()
tracts_plot = tracts_plot.rename(columns={"GEOID": "tract_id"})
tracts_plot["tract_id"] = tracts_plot["tract_id"].astype(str).str.zfill(11)

tracts_plot = tracts_plot.merge(crime_counts, on="tract_id", how="left")
tracts_plot["crime_count"] = tracts_plot["crime_count"].fillna(0).astype(int)

# Convert to GeoJSON-like dict for Altair
geojson_dict = json.loads(tracts_plot.to_json())
```

```{python}
# Choropleth of Crime counts
choropleth = (
    alt.Chart(
        alt.InlineData(
            values=geojson_dict,
            format=alt.DataFormat(type="json", property="features")
        )
    )
    .mark_geoshape(stroke="white", strokeWidth=0.2)
    .encode(
        color=alt.Color(
            "properties.crime_count:Q",
            title="Crime count",
            scale=alt.Scale(scheme="oranges", domainMin=0)
        ),
        tooltip=[
            alt.Tooltip("properties.tract_id:N", title="Tract"),
            alt.Tooltip("properties.crime_count:Q", title="Crime count"),
        ]
    )
    .properties(
        width=350,
        height=350,
        title="Chicago Crime Count by Census Tract (2019–2023)"
    )
    .project(type="mercator")
)

choropleth
```

## Relationship: Crime counts and Income

```{python}
# Subset data
tract_stats = (
    crime_counts
    .merge(income, on="tract_id", how="inner")
    .dropna(subset=["median_hh_income"])
    .copy()
)

# make sure numeric
tract_stats["median_hh_income"] = pd.to_numeric(tract_stats["median_hh_income"], errors="coerce")
tract_stats["crime_count"] = pd.to_numeric(tract_stats["crime_count"], errors="coerce")
tract_stats = tract_stats.dropna(subset=["median_hh_income", "crime_count"])
```

```{python}
# Draw the scatterpoint plot
brush = alt.selection_interval()

# points
points = (
    alt.Chart(tract_stats)
    .mark_circle(size=20, opacity=0.5)
    .encode(
        x=alt.X("median_hh_income:Q", title="Median household income (ACS 5-year, USD)"),
        y=alt.Y("crime_count:Q", title="Crime count (2019–2023)"),
        tooltip=[
            alt.Tooltip("tract_id:N", title="Tract"),
            alt.Tooltip("median_hh_income:Q", title="Income", format=",.0f"),
            alt.Tooltip("crime_count:Q", title="Crime count"),
        ],
        color=alt.condition(brush, alt.value("orange"), alt.value("lightgray"))
    )
    .add_params(brush)
    .properties(width=350, height=250, title="Crime vs Income by Census Tract")
)

# regression line
line = (
    alt.Chart(tract_stats)
    .transform_regression("median_hh_income", "crime_count", method="linear")
    .mark_line(color="red", size=2)
    .encode(
        x="median_hh_income:Q",
        y="crime_count:Q",
    )
)

# combine
scatter = (
    (points + line)
    .properties(
        width=350,
        height=250,
        title="Crime vs Income by Census Tract"
    )
)

scatter
```

# Part 2: LIHTC low-income unit counts and Tracts

## Relationship: LIHTC pis / alloc and Tracts 

```{python}
lihtc_pis["tract_id"].value_counts()


lihtc_pis_counts = (
    lihtc_pis
    .groupby("tract_id", as_index=False)
    .agg(li_units_total=("li_units", "sum"))
)

lihtc_alloc_counts = (
    lihtc_alloc
    .groupby("tract_id", as_index=False)
    .agg(li_units_total=("li_units", "sum"))
)

tracts_plot = tracts_chi.to_crs("EPSG:4326").copy()
tracts_plot = tracts_plot.rename(columns={"GEOID": "tract_id"})
tracts_plot["tract_id"] = tracts_plot["tract_id"].astype(str).str.zfill(11)

tracts_pis = tracts_plot.merge(lihtc_pis_counts, on="tract_id", how="left")
tracts_pis["li_units_total"] = tracts_pis["li_units_total"].fillna(0)

tracts_alloc = tracts_plot.merge(lihtc_alloc_counts, on="tract_id", how="left")
tracts_alloc["li_units_total"] = tracts_alloc["li_units_total"].fillna(0)

geojson_pis = json.loads(tracts_pis.to_json())
geojson_alloc = json.loads(tracts_alloc.to_json())
```

```{python}
map = (
    alt.Chart(
        alt.InlineData(
            values=geojson_pis,
            format=alt.DataFormat(type="json", property="features")
        )
    )
    .mark_geoshape(stroke="white", strokeWidth=0.2)
    .encode(
        color=alt.Color(
            "properties.li_units_total:Q",
            title="LIHTC units (PIS)",
            scale=alt.Scale(scheme="blues", domainMin=0)
        ),
        tooltip=[
            alt.Tooltip("properties.tract_id:N", title="Tract"),
            alt.Tooltip("properties.li_units_total:Q", title="LIHTC units")
        ]
    )
    .properties(
        width=350,
        height=350,
        title="LIHTC Units Placed in Service (2019–2023)"
    )
    .project(type="mercator")
)

map_pis = (
    alt.Chart(
        alt.InlineData(
            values=geojson_pis,
            format=alt.DataFormat(type="json", property="features")
        )
    )
    .mark_geoshape(stroke="white", strokeWidth=0.2)
    .encode(
        color=alt.Color(
            "properties.li_units_total:Q",
            title="LIHTC units (PIS)",
            scale=alt.Scale(scheme="blues", domainMin=0)
        ),
        tooltip=[
            alt.Tooltip("properties.tract_id:N", title="Tract"),
            alt.Tooltip("properties.li_units_total:Q", title="LIHTC units")
        ]
    )
    .properties(
        width=350,
        height=350,
        title="LIHTC Units Placed in Service (2019–2023)"
    )
    .project(type="mercator")
)

map_alloc = (
    alt.Chart(
        alt.InlineData(
            values=geojson_alloc,
            format=alt.DataFormat(type="json", property="features")
        )
    )
    .mark_geoshape(stroke="white", strokeWidth=0.2)
    .encode(
        color=alt.Color(
            "properties.li_units_total:Q",
            title="LIHTC units (Alloc)",
            scale=alt.Scale(scheme="blues", domainMin=0)
        ),
        tooltip=[
            alt.Tooltip("properties.tract_id:N", title="Tract"),
            alt.Tooltip("properties.li_units_total:Q", title="LIHTC units")
        ]
    )
    .properties(
        width=350,
        height=350,
        title="LIHTC Units Allocated (2019–2023)"
    )
    .project(type="mercator")
)

map_pis | map_alloc

lihtc_pis["tract_id"].isin(chi_tract_ids).sum()
```

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import geopandas as gpd
import altair as alt
import json

# 1) Load affordable rental housing CSV
aff = pd.read_csv("data/Affordable_Rental_Housing_Developments_20260303.csv")

# clean units + keep rows with coords
aff["Units"] = pd.to_numeric(aff["Units"], errors="coerce").fillna(0)
aff = aff.dropna(subset=["Latitude", "Longitude"]).copy()

# 2) Make points GeoDataFrame (EPSG:4326)
aff_gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
    aff,
    geometry=gpd.points_from_xy(aff["Longitude"], aff["Latitude"]),
    crs="EPSG:4326",
)

# 3) Prepare Chicago tracts in same CRS + ensure tract_id
tracts_plot = tracts_chi.to_crs("EPSG:4326").copy()
tracts_plot = tracts_plot.rename(columns={"GEOID": "tract_id"})
tracts_plot["tract_id"] = tracts_plot["tract_id"].astype(str).str.zfill(11)

# 4) Spatial join: assign each affordable development point to a tract
# (predicate="within" usually works; if you get too many unmatched, try "intersects")
joined = gpd.sjoin(
    aff_gdf,
    tracts_plot[["tract_id", "geometry"]],
    how="left",
    predicate="within"
)

# 5) Aggregate total Units by tract
units_by_tract = (
    joined.dropna(subset=["tract_id"])
    .groupby("tract_id", as_index=False)["Units"]
    .sum()
    .rename(columns={"Units": "aff_units_total"})
)

# 6) Merge back to tracts for mapping
tracts_units = tracts_plot.merge(units_by_tract, on="tract_id", how="left")
tracts_units["aff_units_total"] = tracts_units["aff_units_total"].fillna(0)

# 7) Altair choropleth (0 = white, then blue gradient)
geojson_dict = json.loads(tracts_units.to_json())

base = (
    alt.Chart(
        alt.InlineData(values=geojson_dict,
                       format=alt.DataFormat(type="json", property="features"))
    )
    .mark_geoshape(fill="transparent", stroke="lightgray", strokeWidth=0.3)
    .properties(width=380, height=380)
    .project(type="mercator")
)

heatmap = (
    alt.Chart(
        alt.InlineData(values=geojson_dict,
                       format=alt.DataFormat(type="json", property="features"))
    )
    .mark_geoshape(stroke="white", strokeWidth=0.2)
    .encode(
        color=alt.Color(
            "properties.aff_units_total:Q",
            title="Affordable units (total)",
            scale=alt.Scale(
                domainMin=0,
                range=["#ffffff", "#deebf7", "#9ecae1", "#3182bd", "#08519c"]
            )
        ),
        tooltip=[
            alt.Tooltip("properties.tract_id:N", title="Tract"),
            alt.Tooltip("properties.aff_units_total:Q", title="Units")
        ]
    )
    .properties(title="Affordable Rental Housing Units by Census Tract (Chicago)")
    .project(type="mercator")
)

(base + heatmap)
```

```{python}
tract_analysis = (
    tracts_units[["tract_id", "aff_units_total"]]
    .merge(crime_counts, on="tract_id", how="left")
)

tract_analysis["crime_count"] = tract_analysis["crime_count"].fillna(0)

scatter = (
    alt.Chart(tract_analysis)
    .mark_circle(size=40, opacity=0.6)
    .encode(
        x=alt.X(
            "aff_units_total:Q",
            title="Affordable Housing Units"
        ),
        y=alt.Y(
            "crime_count:Q",
            title="Crime Count (2019–2023)"
        ),
        tooltip=[
            alt.Tooltip("tract_id:N", title="Tract"),
            alt.Tooltip("aff_units_total:Q", title="Affordable units"),
            alt.Tooltip("crime_count:Q", title="Crime count")
        ]
    )
    .properties(
        width=400,
        height=350,
        title="Crime vs Affordable Housing Units by Census Tract"
    )
)

trend = (
    alt.Chart(tract_analysis)
    .transform_regression("aff_units_total", "crime_count")
    .mark_line()
    .encode(
        x=alt.X("aff_units_total:Q"),
        y=alt.Y("crime_count:Q")
    )
)

scatter + trend

crime_raw.columns
```
